summary: Completed comprehensive testing of dollar mode vs percent mode fix for buy/sell offset calculations. Backend: 62/62 tests passed, confirming AAPL uses absolute target prices (250.00/250.10) in dollar mode and NVDA correctly calculates percentage offsets from average in percent mode. Frontend: All display logic working correctly with proper prefixes (green $ for dollar mode, red dash for percent mode), correct labels, and functional mode toggling via 'Use %' checkbox.

design_issues.issue: Sell input shows blue prefix instead of green $ prefix in dollar mode
design_issues.note: May be intentional design to differentiate buy (green) from sell (blue) actions

passed_tests: Backend GET /api/tickers returns correct buy_percent/sell_percent values for AAPL (false/false) and NVDA (true/true)
Backend AAPL dollar mode configuration verified: buy_offset=250.0, sell_offset=250.1 as absolute target prices
Backend NVDA percent mode configuration verified: buy_offset=-3.0, sell_offset=3.0 as percentage offsets
Backend ticker update API correctly switches between dollar and percent modes
Backend calculation fix confirmed: dollar mode uses absolute values, percent mode uses avg*(1+offset/100)
Frontend AAPL card shows 'Buy: $250.00' and 'Sell: $250.10' in quick stats line
Frontend AAPL expanded view shows 'Buy Price ($)' and 'Sell Price ($)' labels
Frontend AAPL buy input shows green $ prefix in dollar mode
Frontend NVDA card shows 'Buy Offset (%)' label in percent mode
Frontend NVDA buy input shows red dash prefix (–) in percent mode
Frontend 'Use %' checkbox successfully toggles between dollar and percent display modes
Frontend mode toggle updates labels from 'Buy Offset (%)' to 'Buy Price ($)' and vice versa
Frontend mode toggle updates input prefixes from red dash to green $ and vice versa
Frontend all tabs (Positions, History, Logs, Settings, Watchlist) load correctly (regression test)
All backend API endpoints functioning correctly (62/62 tests passed)

success_percentage.frontend: 98% (minor cosmetic issue with sell input prefix color, core functionality perfect)
